title: weinmannia pinnata var. caripensis
intro: Weinmannia pinnata var. caripensis, also known as the Caripén or Red Sap, is a variety of the Weinmannia pinnata tree, which is native to the Andean region of South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its sap, which contains tannins and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Weinmannia pinnata var. caripensis include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, among other uses. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and its sap is used topically to aid in the healing process.
